title: Replication of a reported catalyst-speedup effect under preregistered null conditions
abstract: We replicate a reported catalyst-speedup effect under preregistered null conditions and show that the effect does not persist relative to the original baseline assay. The manuscript emphasizes constraint, replication logic, and transparent reporting.
manuscript_text: We rerun the original assay with the preregistered null workflow and the same baseline catalyst conditions. Table 1 reports the replicated speed measurements and uncertainty intervals. Figure 1 shows that the originally reported effect does not persist under the replication workflow. We release the assay protocol, raw tables, and analysis scripts.
figures_and_captions: Figure 1. Replication result showing that the reported speedup does not persist.
tables: Table 1. Replicated speed measurements with uncertainty intervals.
supplement_or_appendix: Appendix A includes the preregistered assay workflow and release checklist.
data_availability: Raw assay tables are publicly archived.
materials_availability: Assay workflow and catalyst sourcing details are in the appendix.
ethics_and_disclosures: The authors declare no competing interests.
